name: Apple-Maple-Caramel-Cream Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds golden delicious apples, peeled and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Roll pastry dough on a well-floured surface into a 12-inch circle. Remove excess flour.
2. Transfer to a 9-inch pie pan; trim to a 1/2-inch overhang. Crimp edges to form a stand-up rim. Prick the bottom of the dough all over with a fork.
3. Refrigerate at least 30 minutes.
4. Preheat oven to 450°F (232°C).
5. Line the crust with aluminum foil and weigh it down with dried beans or pie weights. Bake for 10 minutes.
6. Reduce temperature to 350°F (177°C). Bake an additional 10 minutes.
7. Remove foil and weights. Bake until crust is golden, about 15 minutes. Cool on a rack.
8. Melt butter in a large skillet. Add apples and sugar; cook, stirring, until apples are very tender and thickly glazed, about 15-20 minutes. Let cool to room temperature.
9. Warm milk in a saucepan until small bubbles form around the edge. Whisk together egg yolk, maple syrup, cornstarch, and vanilla in a bowl until smooth.
10. Gradually whisk in warm milk into the egg mixture. Return to saucepan. Stir over low heat until thickened. Remove from heat; whisk for 1 minute.
11. Spoon apples into crust. Pour hot maple mixture over top. Smooth surface. Let cool to room temperature.
12. Refrigerate until custard is firm, about 1 hour.
13. Beat heavy cream in a bowl until soft peaks form. Add sugar and cinnamon; beat until stiff peaks form.
14. Serve with whipped cream decorated on top.
